campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review how the city's money was spent and invested during July 2025, checking the monthly financial reports. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including how different types of transportation (cars, buses, bikes, etc.) will work together.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment transactions and where city money was placed
Monthly treasurer's financial report for July 2025
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit, biking, and pedestrian options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from the public, the council decided to move forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first formal reading, so they can vote on adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Fast-tracked approval process used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work weren't fully outlined in the available information.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement
Review of closed session discussions from March meetings

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed updates on programs designed to help young people in the community, including youth empowerment initiatives and a summer safety program. They also discussed how the city is enforcing building and housing codes, and shared plans for improving library buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and youth intervention programs progress
Safe Summer Initiative Grant funding and activities
Code Enforcement annual report on housing and building violations
Library facility upgrades and customer service improvements
Children and Youth Services Master Plan implementation status

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included a virtual option for residents who wanted to participate online. The meeting allowed community members to join either in person or via Zoom video conference and phone to speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting at 7 P.M. with a flag salute. Three commissioners shared personal stories about their experiences in Sunnyvale, and the group approved the official notes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Commissioner personal stories about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from the previous month and opened the floor for residents to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 using a hybrid format, meaning residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city provided multiple ways for the public to participate, including video conference and phone options, and allowed residents to submit written comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote attendance)
Zoom participation details and meeting access information
Public comment submission procedures via eComment system
Remote participation phone line access

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning documents for 2025-2026, including updates to the city's federal funding plan and new project requests from council members. They also discussed changes to how the city fills council vacancies and approved meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Updates to city's 5-year federal funding and housing plan (2025-2030)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to council vacancy filling process
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the gas utility's financial plan for the next fiscal year and adjust residential and commercial gas rates accordingly. The meeting included approval of previous meeting minutes and a staff presentation about the utility's reserves and budget needs.
keyTopics: Gas rate changes for homes and businesses
Gas utility budget and financial forecast for 2027
Utility reserve fund transfers
Impact on residential, multi-unit, and large commercial gas customers
